Task readiness tools (list_ready_tasks, suggest_next_task) live on gobby-tasks. Agent messaging on gobby-agents uses: send_message(from_session, to_session, content) for P2P messaging (replaces send_to_child/send_to_parent), deliver_pending_messages(session_id) for fetching undelivered messages and marking as delivered (replaces poll_messages + mark_message_read), send_command/complete_command/activate_command for structured command flow. broadcast_to_children was removed with no replacement.

Provider inherit resolution: SessionSource values mostly match provider names directly. Only 3 divergent cases need normalization: claude_sdk -> claude, claude_sdk_web_chat -> claude, antigravity -> claude. Everything else (claude, gemini, codex, cursor, windsurf, copilot) passes through as-is. Don't create identity maps — just normalize the exceptions. For mode:self agents, provider inherit stays unresolved since no process is spawned.

Agent cards in AgentsTab.tsx use `agent-def-card` CSS class (not `workflows-card` like Pipelines/Rules). The agent card has no padding — content padding comes from `.agent-def-header` (16px) and `.agent-def-card > .workflows-card-footer` (10px 16px 16px). The `isDb` variable (`item.source.endsWith('-db')`) was previously used to gate Edit/Delete buttons, but installed agents have source='installed' with valid db_id, so the condition was changed to just check `item.db_id`. Pipelines and Rules always show Delete for non-template items.

## Agents v3 Design Decisions (Epic #9085)

### Core Concepts
- **Selectors**: Compact string syntax for targeting rules by metadata — `tag:X`, `group:X`, `name:X`, `source:X`, `*`. Fnmatch globs. Include is OR, exclude wins on conflict. No boolean combinators in v3.
- **`extends`**: Single inheritance for agent definitions. Child overrides parent. Max depth 5, cycle detection. Uses Pydantic `model_fields_set` to detect explicit overrides vs defaults.
- **`inherit`**: Sentinel value for provider/model. Resolved from CLI source at session_start or spawn time via `_CLI_SOURCE_TO_PROVIDER` map.
- **`mode: self`**: "Persona, not process." Loads rules+variables onto current session without spawning. Already exists in spawn layer but not in `AgentDefinitionBody.mode` Literal. Being added as first-class + decoupled from requiring a workflow.
- **`default_agent`**: New `DaemonConfig` field (default: "default"). Deep loaded at session_start in `handle_session_start` — before rules evaluate. Writes `_agent_type`, `_active_rule_names`, preset variables to `session_variables`.
- **`_active_rule_names`**: New session variable. When set, rule engine filters to only these rule names (via `_filter_by_active_rules`). When None, all rules pass (backward compat).

### Key Architecture
- New files: `src/gobby/workflows/selectors.py`, `src/gobby/workflows/agent_resolver.py`
- Injection point: `src/gobby/hooks/event_handlers/_session.py` handle_session_start, after session registration, before rules evaluate
- Rule engine change: `_filter_by_active_rules` added after `_filter_by_agent_scope` in evaluate()
- Spawn evolution: `_modes.py` gets `_handle_self_persona` for workflow-less mode:self
- Agent sync already exists at `src/gobby/agents/sync.py`

### Phase 2: Variable Definitions
- First-class `workflow_type='variable'` in `workflow_definitions` table (no schema change)
- Replaces 14 session-defaults rules (all pure set_variable initializers guarded by `is None`)
- 6 conditional set_variable rules in other groups stay as rules
- User chose first-class variable registry over variables-as-rule-outputs

### User Preferences
- User wants `mode: self` (not terminal/headless) for the baseline agent concept
- User prefers config setting for default agent activation (not convention-based)
- User wants deep load pattern (like session ID) for agent activation at session_start
- Keep straightforward for agents v3 — no boolean combinators, no compound selectors

In gobby init rules, each `set_variable` effect can only set one variable. To initialize multiple variables (e.g., `task_claimed` and `task_ref`), you must create separate init rules—one per variable, following the naming pattern `init-{variable-name}.yaml` containing a rule named `init-{variable-name}`.

E2E test daemon startup was failing because load_config Phase 2 started from empty dict when DB was empty, losing all config settings (ports, test_mode, etc). Fixed by layering: bootstrap.yaml -> config.yaml -> DB -> Pydantic defaults. The E2E conftest must write both bootstrap.yaml (for ports/db_path) and config.yaml (for test_mode, memory settings, etc). The conftest passes --config pointing to config.yaml; load_bootstrap resolves bootstrap.yaml from the same directory. 15 remaining E2E failures are stale tests referencing gobby-orchestration server and removed inter-agent messaging tools (send_to_parent, send_to_child, poll_messages) - tracked in #9083 and #9084.

Config loading has 3 layers in Phase 2 (load_config with config_store): Layer 1: bootstrap.yaml (5 pre-DB fields: daemon_port, database_path, bind_host, websocket_port, ui_port). Layer 2: config.yaml in same dir as bootstrap (non-bootstrap settings like test_mode, memory, logging). Layer 3: DB config_store (runtime overrides). Each layer deep-merges on top of previous. Phase 1 (without config_store) only loads bootstrap.yaml. E2E tests must create bootstrap.yaml alongside config.yaml with ports and db_path for daemon to bind to correct ports. The VectorStore qdrant_path fallback in runner.py uses GOBBY_HOME env var (not hardcoded Path.home() / '.gobby').

Bug fix: memory-review-gate never clears after create_memory. Root cause: Claude Code's post-tool-use (after_tool) hook omits tool_input, so normalization falls back to prefix parsing yielding mcp_server='gobby' and mcp_tool='call_tool' instead of 'gobby-memory'/'create_memory'. Fix: changed clear-memory-review-on-create rule from after_tool to before_tool event (pre-tool-use always includes tool_input). Also increased max_stop_attempts from 3 to 8 and made memory-review-gate use the max_stop_attempts variable instead of hardcoded 3.

Workflows page filter pattern: Filters are managed in WorkflowsPage.tsx as lifted state and passed to FilterPopover (for rendering toggles/chips) and to each tab component (RulesTab, PipelinesTab, AgentsTab) as props. Each tab has its own `installedNames` useMemo (Set of installed item names from unfiltered data) and a filtered list useMemo that applies all filter predicates. When adding a new filter toggle: (1) add state in WorkflowsPage, (2) update activeFilterCount badge, (3) pass to FilterPopover and tabs, (4) add FilterPopover props + conditional rendering, (5) add prop to each tab interface + filter step in the filtered memo. The hideInstalled toggle is conditionally rendered only when sourceFilter === 'templates'.

Gobby has no built-in CLI or MCP command to clear all agent runs at once. The 'gobby agents cleanup' command only removes stale/old runs. To completely clear agent run history, directly access the database and execute DELETE FROM agent_runs; (the agent_runs table stores all run records).

The filtering logic uses source != 'template' to exclude system rules, but this only works after the bundled→template migration runs. Running the daemon without triggering the migration will cause 'bundled' rules to bypass filters incorrectly.

Rules and workflows contain a 'source' field with three values: 'custom' (user-defined), 'bundled' (pre-migration system default), 'template' (post-migration). The daemon migration on restart converts source='bundled' → source='template'.

Linter revert behavior: When editing web/src/components files, if WorkflowsPage.tsx has TypeScript type mismatches with child component props (e.g. missing required props like enabledFilter, filterProvider, eventFilter), the linter will revert ALL modified files in the web/src/components directory back to their git HEAD state. To avoid this, always write WorkflowsPage.tsx (the parent) BEFORE editing child tab components, or apply all edits atomically. The linter runs after each Write/Edit tool call.

Unified Filter Button for Workflows Page: Consolidated filter row (search, source dropdown, hide built-in toggle) and per-tab chip bars into a single Filter button with popover in WorkflowsPage.tsx. Tab-specific filter state (pipelineEnabledFilter, agentProviderFilter, ruleEventFilter) is lifted to WorkflowsPage and passed as props. Dynamic options (agentProviders, ruleEventTypes) are reported by tabs via callbacks. Bulk actions (Install All, Enable All) moved to toolbar-right. FilterPopover is a local component in WorkflowsPage.tsx with Source chips, tab-specific section, and Hide Built-in toggle. Click-outside closes popover. Badge shows active filter count. CSS uses .workflows-filter-btn, .workflows-filter-badge, .workflows-filter-popover classes. Old .workflows-source-select CSS removed.

Gobby task/session ID resolution: Short IDs like #1802 or #9080 sometimes fail with 'not found in project' errors. Using full UUIDs (e.g., '35768d76-c7ee-4922-8fb7-7dd6e571af84' for tasks, '6b74ef26-0722-46e8-8a61-6a35b0d0e1f3' for sessions) works reliably. Also, create_task with session_id as short form (#1802) fails with FOREIGN KEY constraint — must use full UUID for session_id. The create_task call itself returned the UUID and seq_num, but close_task couldn't find the task by #seq_num — needed the full UUID.

Plugin system removal: The plugin system was removed in #8827, but the `plugins` CLI command group (list, reload) in src/gobby/cli/extensions.py was left behind. Task #9080 removes it. The ClaudePluginsProvider files are NOT part of the plugin system — they are the skill hub system (claude-plugins.dev API) and must stay.

The clear-memory-review-on-create rule in memory-lifecycle templates has enabled: false by design (all templates do). The memory-review-gate stop hook fires when pending_memory_review is set, but the clearing rule only works if it's been installed and enabled in the DB. When implementing rules that depend on other rules clearing variables, verify the dependent rule is actually active.

Tool normalization architecture: `normalize_tool_fields()` in `src/gobby/hooks/normalization.py` is the primary entry point for all CLI adapters. It runs two phases: (1) field alias normalization (function_name→tool_name, toolName→tool_name, toolArgs→tool_input with JSON parsing, parameters→tool_input, args→tool_input, mcp_context→mcp_server/mcp_tool), (2) delegates to `normalize_mcp_fields()` for mcp__ prefix parsing, call_tool inner extraction, and tool_result/tool_response→tool_output. All 6 adapters (Claude Code, Gemini, Cursor, Copilot, Windsurf, Codex) plus web chat now use this shared function. Cursor has additional handling for beforeMCPExecution/afterMCPExecution hooks (JSON string parsing + MCP server/tool extraction from tool_input + result_json→tool_output). Windsurf's _extract_tool_info uses mcp_server_name/mcp_tool_name with fallback to server_name/tool_name.

When Antigravity encounters a Fail/Failure to Send error, switch the model from Gemini Pro to Gemini Flash, send a prompt, then switch back to Gemini Pro. This workaround resolves the connection failure.

Chat UI rules engine parity: MCP normalization is now shared between CLI adapter and web-chat via hooks/normalization.py:normalize_mcp_fields(). MCP call effects from rules are dispatched via hooks/mcp_dispatch.py:dispatch_mcp_calls() (async-native). Direct websocket tool_call messages now fire BEFORE_TOOL/AFTER_TOOL hooks in handlers.py. SESSION_START fires after _create_chat_session(), SESSION_END fires before cleanup in clear_chat, delete_chat, idle cleanup, and server stop(). Session context enrichment (Gobby Session ID: #N) is injected in _fire_lifecycle after workflow evaluation.

The stop-gates rule reset-stop-on-native-tool only reset stop_attempts on native (non-MCP) tool calls. This caused the require-task-close rule to not fire when agents performed MCP calls between stop cycles, because stop_attempts stayed elevated. Fixed by replacing it with reset-stop-on-any-tool which has no `when` condition and fires on all after_tool events. Old rule moved to rules/deprecated/.

list_rules MCP tool supports brief=True parameter (added in #9074). When brief=True, returns only name/event/group/enabled per rule instead of the full summary (which includes id, description, effect, when, priority, source, tags). This reduces token output from ~20k to a fraction. Implementation: _rule_brief() helper in src/gobby/mcp_proxy/tools/workflows/_rules.py, wired through __init__.py registry.

PipelinesTab dropdown bug fix: The '+ Pipeline' dropdown was rendering inside PipelinesTab's filter bar instead of inside the .workflows-new-wrapper div in WorkflowsPage. CSS expects position:absolute dropdown as child of position:relative wrapper. Fix: moved dropdown to WorkflowsPage, PipelinesTab now receives createMode prop ('builder'|'yaml'|null) and reacts via useEffect.

Playwright MCP server must be configured as stdio transport (command: npx, args: ["-y", "@playwright/mcp@latest"]), NOT as HTTP. Previous broken config had it as HTTP pointing to localhost:8931/sse and /mcp which doesn't work. The sidebar nav buttons can be outside the viewport in Playwright - use browser_evaluate with scrollIntoView().click() as a workaround.

Bug fix #9071: Stop hook fails to block when agent has claimed task. Root cause: dual-write desync between workflow_states and session_variables. claim_task/create_task wrote task_claimed=True to workflow_states only, but the rule engine evaluates session_variables with higher precedence. If session_variables has stale task_claimed=false from init-task-claimed at session start, it overrides the truth. Fix: mirror writes to session_variables via ctx.session_var_manager.merge_variables() in claim_task, close_task, and create_task(claim=True). Also added SessionVariableManager to RegistryContext in _context.py. Secondary fix: removed [:8] truncated UUID usage in 5 places across task tools (_lifecycle.py, _crud.py, _session.py, _expansion.py).

Convention: Never use truncated UUIDs (e.g. task_id[:8]) for task_ref or any other purpose. The system can't resolve truncated UUIDs back to tasks, and there's collision risk. Use #seq_num (e.g. #47) as the preferred human-readable ref, or fall back to the full UUID. Josh considers truncated UUIDs sloppy architecture.

When using replace_all in JSX, watch for nested expression contexts. Replacing a button inside {condition && (...)} with {ternary ? ... : ...} creates invalid double-brace nesting like {condition && ({ternary ...})}. The fix is to remove the outer braces from the replacement so it becomes {condition && (ternary ? ... : ...)}. Always run tsc --noEmit after JSX replace_all operations.

Install button UX pattern for Pipelines/Rules/Agents tabs: derive an `installedNames` Set from the full data array (not filtered), checking source==='installed' && !deleted_at. Then in template cards, use a ternary: installedNames.has(name) renders a disabled 'Installed' button, otherwise the clickable 'Install' button. For RulesTab, pass isInstalled as a prop to the RuleCard component since it's a separate function.

Watchdog circuit breaker state is now persisted to ~/.gobby/watchdog_state.json. The _load_state()/_save_state() methods read/write restart_times as JSON. This means the circuit breaker survives watchdog process restarts — new watchdog instances inherit previous restart counts instead of starting fresh. Tests must mock get_gobby_home during Watchdog construction to avoid loading production state. failure_threshold default changed from 3 to 5.
psutil: proc.connections() is deprecated. Use proc.net_connections() instead. Fixed in src/gobby/cli/utils.py (kill_all_gobby_daemons and _kill_port_holder).
Task #9067 tracks the removal of the remaining plugin source code.
Plugin-related registrations still exist in __init__.py and runner.py.
ClaudePluginsProvider still exists at src/gobby/skills/hubs/claude_plugins.py.
CLI commands for extensions still exist at src/gobby/cli/extensions.py.
The plugin system removal was incomplete, with remnants still existing in the codebase.
MCP tools replace plugins as the extension mechanism in the project.
The plugin system was removed per the workflows-v2 plan documented in docs/plans/workflows-v2.md.

The plugin system was removed per the workflows-v2 plan (docs/plans/workflows-v2.md). MCP tools replace plugins as the extension mechanism. However, the removal was incomplete — CLI commands (src/gobby/cli/extensions.py), ClaudePluginsProvider (src/gobby/skills/hubs/claude_plugins.py), and registrations in __init__.py and runner.py still exist. Task #9067 tracks the source removal.

chat.py websocket module (src/gobby/servers/websocket/chat.py) uses `from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any` for type imports. When adding typed dict parameters, `Any` is already available. The `_notify_plan_ready` callback on line 176 takes `input_data: dict[str, Any]` for plan approval data containing allowedPrompts.

Testing pattern for close_task MCP proxy behavior: to test workflow state variable clearing in close_task, patch SessionTaskManager, LocalSessionManager, WorkflowStateManager, and LocalProjectManager in gobby.mcp_proxy.tools.tasks._context. Create a real WorkflowState with task_claimed=True and claimed_task_id set, assign it to mock_ws_manager.get_state.return_value, then assert the state.variables dict is mutated after the call. See test_close_task_clears_task_claimed_variables in tests/mcp_proxy/tools/test_tasks_coverage.py.

Bug #9064: close_task in _lifecycle.py failed to clear task_claimed/claimed_task_id session variables after successful close. Root cause: the claimed_task_id comparison and clearing logic was outside the `if state:` block where claimed_task_id was defined, and the outer `except Exception: pass` silently swallowed any errors. Fix: moved comparison+clearing inside `if state:`, added UUID normalization for robust comparison, replaced silent exception swallowing with logger.warning(). File: src/gobby/mcp_proxy/tools/tasks/_lifecycle.py lines ~250-290. The observer fallback in observers.py intentionally does NOT clear for Claude Code (no tool_output in AFTER_TOOL hooks), so CC users are entirely dependent on this MCP proxy clearing code.

IMPORTANT: Files in src/gobby/install/shared/ are TEMPLATES with enabled: false. They are NOT active enforcement. The installed copies in the workflow_definitions DB table (with source='installed') are what's actually active. Always check the DB state, not the template YAML files, when debugging rule behavior. This is documented in CLAUDE.md under 'Templates vs Active Enforcement'.

Mobile responsive CSS added to WorkflowsPage.css and ConfigurationPage.css using @media (max-width: 768px) breakpoint, consistent with DashboardPage, McpPage, CronJobsPage. Patterns: flex-wrap for filter rows, single-column grid, horizontal scroll for filter chips, full-width modals. The 'gobby' tag is injected at sync time (not in YAML source files) in workflows/sync.py and agents/sync.py via _ensure_gobby_tag_on_installed() helper. The 'Hide Built-in' toggle in WorkflowsPage filters items by this tag across all 3 tabs.

WorkflowsPage layout was restructured: title row ("Workflows" + context-aware create button) is separate from the filter row (search, Hide Built-in toggle, source select, refresh). The enforcement kill switch (rules.enforcement_enabled) was moved from RulesTab to ConfigurationPage as a 'Rules Engine' section at the top of the config form tab. useConfiguration hook gained rulesEnforcement + setRulesEnforcement. The RulesTab no longer has the enforcement toggle - only the bulk Enable All toggle for installed/project rules remains.

Gobby tag system for built-in templates: All bundled templates (rules, workflows, agents) are tagged with 'gobby' at sync time in sync_bundled_rules(), sync_bundled_workflows(), and sync_bundled_agents(). The tag is injected in the sync functions (not in YAML source files) so it automatically applies to all current and future templates. A _ensure_gobby_tag_on_installed() helper patches existing installed copies missing the tag. The frontend has a 'Hide Built-in' toggle in WorkflowsPage toolbar that filters items with the 'gobby' tag across all three tabs.

MCP call_tool unwrapping bug fix: The rule engine's _build_eval_context() unwraps call_tool arguments (replacing tool_input with the inner 'arguments' dict), but this stripped server_name and tool_name needed by is_tool_unlocked/is_discovery_tool helpers. Fixed by re-injecting MCP routing fields (server_name, tool_name) into the unwrapped dict after unwrapping. Also removed the now-redundant tool_input.get('arguments') check from require-schema-before-call.yaml's when condition.

Efficient bulk fix for test files calling get_by_name() without include_templates=True: Instead of modifying every individual get_by_name() call (74+ across 6 files), modify the _sync_bundled() helper to execute `db.execute("UPDATE workflow_definitions SET source = 'installed' WHERE source = 'template'")` after syncing. This is a one-line-per-file change that fixes ALL get_by_name() calls in that file. For files with RuleEngine integration tests (e.g. test_progressive_disclosure_rules.py), the engine fixture must also `db.execute("UPDATE workflow_definitions SET enabled = 0")` before selectively enabling test rules, to prevent interference from other rules that may have been synced with enabled=True in their YAML.

AgentDefinitionBody isolation field uses Literal['none', 'worktree', 'clone'] | None. The value 'current' was replaced with 'none' in the model. Tests referencing 'current' need to be updated to 'none'.

Pre-existing bug: require-schema-before-call rule's when condition evaluates to allow (not block) in RuleEngine integration tests. The condition uses `is_tool_unlocked(tool_input)` and `is_discovery_tool(tool_input.get('tool_name'))` helper functions. The rule works at the YAML/Pydantic level but fails in RuleEngine.evaluate() - likely the helper functions aren't registered in the engine's evaluator context. Affects 3 tests in TestRuleEngineIntegration in test_progressive_disclosure_rules.py: test_call_tool_blocked_before_schema_lookup, test_full_disclosure_flow, test_full_round_trip_via_rule_engine. The get_tool_schema blocking tests pass, suggesting the issue is specific to `is_tool_unlocked`/`is_discovery_tool` helpers.

Fix for bundled rule tests failing with get_by_name() returning None: Bundled rules are synced with source='template'. Tests calling get_by_name() without include_templates=True get None. Fix: modify _sync_bundled() helper in each test file to execute `db.execute("UPDATE workflow_definitions SET source = 'installed' WHERE source = 'template'")` after syncing. This marks templates as installed so get_by_name() finds them. CAUTION: For files with RuleEngine integration tests, this can cause interference if other rules were synced with enabled=True. In that case, the engine fixture must disable all rules first (`UPDATE workflow_definitions SET enabled = 0`) before enabling only the rules under test.
get_gobby_home() is defined in gobby.cli.utils, NOT in gobby.config.bootstrap. The auth.py module (#9055 commit) had a wrong import path that broke all CLI commands. Fixed in #9058.

Session #1775 completed the Agent System Improvements plan across all 4 sections: (1) Removed TerminalType enum and terminal field from entire codebase - everything uses tmux, (2) Replaced old form grid with two-column AgentEditForm.tsx component, (3) Renamed isolation 'current' to 'none' everywhere + added worktree_id reuse + IsolationTargetSelector.tsx, (4) Added update_agent_rules/update_agent_variables MCP tools + PATCH endpoints + AgentRulesEditor.tsx chips + AgentVariablesEditor.tsx key-value editor. All 227 tests pass, mypy clean, tsc clean. New files: web/src/components/agents/{AgentEditForm,IsolationTargetSelector,AgentRulesEditor,AgentVariablesEditor}.tsx. ~30 files modified across backend and frontend.

Frontend agent editor was refactored: AgentEditForm.tsx (two-column layout), AgentRulesEditor.tsx (chip-based), AgentVariablesEditor.tsx (key-value), IsolationTargetSelector.tsx (worktree/clone picker) were created in web/src/components/agents/. AgentsTab.tsx was updated to use these components, remove terminal references, update isolation from 'current' to 'none', and the old ModelSelector component was removed (now embedded in AgentEditForm). The workflows type in AgentDefInfo changed from Record<string, WorkflowSummary> to { pipeline?: string, rules?: string[], variables?: Record<string, unknown>, [key: string]: unknown }.

Agent definition CRUD now includes update_agent_rules and update_agent_variables MCP tools (registered in agents.py) and PATCH /api/agents/definitions/{id}/rules and /variables HTTP endpoints (in routes/agents.py). The MCP tools are in agent_definitions.py. Rules uses add/remove lists, variables uses set_vars dict and remove list. Both modify the workflows.rules and workflows.variables fields in definition_json.

worktree_id reuse was added to spawn_agent, mirroring the existing clone_id pattern. In _implementation.py, priority is: worktree_id > clone_id > fresh isolation. When worktree_id is provided, it looks up the worktree from storage, constructs an IsolationContext from it, and skips the handler. The _factory.py spawn_agent tool now accepts worktree_id: str | None parameter.

Isolation mode 'current' was renamed to 'none' across the entire codebase. CurrentIsolationHandler was renamed to NoneIsolationHandler. The Literal type in definitions.py, isolation.py, _factory.py, _implementation.py all use Literal['none', 'worktree', 'clone']. Default fallbacks changed from 'current' to 'none'. Mode=self override uses 'none' instead of 'current'.

TerminalType enum was removed from the agent spawner system. Everything uses tmux now (TmuxSpawner). The `terminal` field was removed from: SpawnRequest, AgentConfig, AgentDefinitionBody API requests, spawn_agent MCP tool params, and dry_run evaluate_spawn. The `terminal_type` property on TerminalSpawnerBase now returns plain str instead of TerminalType enum. In registry.py, _close_terminal_window was gutted to only keep tmux kill-pane, parent_pid fallback, and Windows taskkill strategies (removed kitty, alacritty, iTerm, Terminal.app, Ghostty, generic pgrep strategies).

gobby-voice MCP tool registry created at src/gobby/mcp_proxy/tools/voice.py. Follows the config registry pattern (same deps: config, config_store, config_setter). Provides 4 tools: add_vocab, remove_vocab, list_vocab, clear_vocab for managing Whisper custom vocabulary. Wired in registries.py after the config registry block. Vocabulary persisted via ConfigStore.set('voice.whisper_vocabulary', terms). InternalToolRegistry.get_tool() returns the function directly (not an object with .handler), so tests should call tool(**kwargs) not tool.handler(**kwargs).

Testing auth routes pattern: Use tests/servers/conftest.py:create_http_server() with real DaemonConfig + real temp_db fixture + real SecretStore. Store test passwords via ConfigStore.set_secret('auth.password', password, secret_store). Use starlette.testclient.TestClient(server.app) for HTTP assertions. TestClient automatically handles cookies across requests within same client instance (important for login→status→logout flows). For fresh-cookie tests, create a new TestClient instance.

config_store.py secret detection gotcha: is_secret_key_name() checks if the LAST segment of a dotted key ends with a suffix from _SECRET_SUFFIXES. For 'auth.password', the last segment is 'password' — it does NOT end with '_password' (underscore prefix). Had to add bare 'password' (without underscore) to _SECRET_SUFFIXES to fix this. Any future secret fields whose last segment IS the suffix name (not prefixed with underscore) need the bare form in the list.

Auth system architecture: Web UI auth uses Fernet encryption (SecretStore) for password storage, NOT PBKDF2 hashing. The password flows through ConfigStore.set_secret() like any other secret. is_secret_key_name() detects 'auth.password' because _SECRET_SUFFIXES includes bare 'password' (added in #9055). Auth routes do simple plaintext comparison against the Fernet-decrypted stored password. The CLI command is `gobby auth` (with --remove flag to disable). Frontend uses useAuth hook + LoginPage component with auth guard in App.tsx.

Cleaned up .gobby/plans/ directory — removed 11 stale plan files left behind by previous agent sessions. These were orphaned artifacts from completed work (progressive disclosure rules, auth system, memory tab crash fix, etc). Committed as 1f88056d on 0.2.21 branch and pushed.

Session #1770 triage of 6 orphaned tasks: Closed #9019 (mypy fixes, commit fc8d702b), #9020 (plan file write fix, commit fd0dd823), #9034 (LLM audit implemented in #9044 commit 5212a0d6), #8998 (duplicate of #9051). Updated #9025 with partial completion (test_definitions green, remaining failures blocked by #9051). Created #9053 for 6 new mypy regressions in chat_session.py, registries.py, auth.py, runner.py. Key remaining work: #9051 (sync_bundled_rules DB population) blocks both #9025 and #9051's 27 test failures.

sync_bundled_rules() root cause: Tasks #9025, #9051, and (closed duplicate) #8998 all share a single root cause — sync_bundled_rules() is not correctly inserting bundled YAML templates into the workflow_definitions DB table. Tests that call sync_bundled_rules() then query for rules by name get None. This affects 27+ tests across test_memory_lifecycle_rules.py, test_tool_hygiene_rules.py, and test_sync-related tests. Uncommitted changes to storage/workflow_definitions.py and storage/migrations.py appear to be in-progress work toward a fix but are not resolving it yet. The workflow_definitions storage layer is the critical path.

workflow_definitions.source has 3 DB values: 'template' (bundled from install/shared/, not executed), 'installed' (global scope, project_id IS NULL), 'project' (project-scoped, has project_id). Migration 124 consolidated 'imported'→'installed' and 'installed'+project_id→'project'. The move_to_project/move_to_global methods on LocalWorkflowDefinitionManager handle scope changes. HTTP endpoints: POST /api/workflows/{id}/move-to-project and /move-to-global. CLI/MCP filesystem display uses 'installed' (was 'global') for non-project workflows.

Workflow System Audit (Feb 2026) — Key findings:

1. P0: SDK/chat path (chat.py _fire_lifecycle()) drops mcp_call effects — 17 enabled rules silently fail in web chat (memory recall, digest, handoff, sync). Reference impl: hook_manager.py _dispatch_mcp_calls().

2. P0: SDK path never emits SESSION_START event — 28 enabled rules never fire in web chat (session-defaults init, context injection, sync imports).

3. P2: SDK path missing webhook dispatch and incomplete mcp_tool field parsing (only parses call_tool, not other mcp__* tools). Mitigated by tool_name fallback in current rules.

4. Rules system is active: 163 total rules in DB (82 templates + 81 installed). ~75 installed rules are enabled and enforcing. 7 intentionally disabled (worker-safety too aggressive, auto-task not ready).

5. Templates vs active enforcement documented in CLAUDE.md and src/gobby/install/shared/CLAUDE.md (commit 6d1d571b).

6. Pipelines and Agents are fully wired (backend + MCP tools + frontend) but untested in production.

7. Conductor was removed; no background orchestration exists yet.

Artifact panel editing state architecture: isEditing and showSource state are owned by ArtifactPanel (parent), not by ArtifactCodeView/ArtifactTextView (children). The Edit/View and Source/Preview toggle buttons live in the ArtifactPanel header row as icon-sized buttons alongside Copy, Download, and Close. ArtifactCodeView and ArtifactTextView accept isEditing and showSource as props. This eliminates the full-width button bar that each child component previously rendered. Show Edit/View when onUpdateContent is defined (editable). Show Source/Preview only for read-only text artifacts (type=text, no onChange).

Plan approval sentence collision fix pattern: The streaming loop in chat.py uses a single assistant_message_id per turn. When ExitPlanMode blocks in the permission handler (waiting for user approval), pre-approval and post-approval text share the same message_id, causing concatenation in the frontend. Fix: add a `_plan_approval_completed` bool flag on ChatSession, set it when approval decision is 'approve' in chat_session_permissions.py. In the TextChunk handler in chat.py, check this flag before the after_tool_call check — if set, flush accumulated text, mint a new assistant_message_id, and reset tracking state. Frontend should NOT eagerly clear planPendingApproval in approvePlan/requestPlanChanges — instead rely on mode_changed WebSocket message as the single source of truth. Also broadcast mode_changed with reason 'plan_changes_requested' on deny path so frontend can dismiss the panel.

CRITICAL DISTINCTION — Templates vs Active Workflows:

- Files in src/gobby/install/shared/ (rules/, workflows/, agents/, pipelines/) are TEMPLATES. They are bundled with the software but are NOT active enforcement.
- Templates have enabled: false BY DESIGN. This is correct and intentional.
- A workflow/rule/pipeline/agent must be INSTALLED (synced to the workflow_definitions DB table) AND ENABLED for it to be active in the rules engine.
- The sync process (src/gobby/workflows/sync.py) copies templates into the DB but preserves the enabled state from the YAML (false).
- The user enables specific rules incrementally via the admin UI or API.
- DO NOT treat 'all 82 rules are disabled' as an issue — that's the expected state for templates.
- DO NOT confuse template files with the active enforcement system.
- The workflow_definitions table has been wiped multiple times; the DB state is the source of truth, not the YAML files.
- The deprecated/ subdirectories are correctly excluded from sync and are irrelevant to current state.

EnterPlanMode/ExitPlanMode flow is working correctly as of 2026-02-23. Tested via task #9039 (trivial unused import cleanup). The flow correctly: entered plan mode, allowed read-only exploration (Read, Glob, Grep), allowed writing plan to .gobby/plans/, exited plan mode with user approval, then was ready to execute. No issues observed.

useChat.ts currentModeRef was initialized to 'accept_edits' but the actual default mode (set in useSettings.ts:22 and chat_session.py:93) is 'plan'. Fixed in #9049 to 'plan'. This ref is used for internal tracking only (the false-positive setPlanPendingApproval fallback that read it was also removed in the same commit). The frontend default mode is sent to the backend on every conversation switch via App.tsx:376-377.

SECURITY FIX: ExitPlanMode timeout was fail-open (auto-approved after 600s), which allowed an agent to build a full auth system without user approval when combined with the PlanApprovalBar being hidden by an isStreaming guard. The root cause chain: (1) PlanApprovalBar hidden by !isStreaming guard (fixed in #9048), (2) 600s timeout auto-approved (fixed in #9049 to fail-closed with request_changes). Design principle: all permission timeouts in chat_session_permissions.py should fail-closed (deny/request_changes), never fail-open. Tool approval timeout already correctly failed closed (line 375: reject). The ExitPlanMode timeout was the exception.

Bug: memory-review-gate hook fires repeatedly on /stop even after memories have already been saved in the session. The hook has no mechanism to check whether create_memory was already called — it just unconditionally blocks stop. Needs a session-scoped flag or a check against recent memory creation timestamps.

Session #1760 summary: Implemented 3 commits on branch 0.2.21. (1) ab65468e [#9046]: Chat UI fixes - stable ChatInput button container (Send left, Stop right), ArtifactTextView Edit/View toggle with CodeMirror, PlanApprovalBar in ArtifactPanel, plan artifacts as type 'text'. (2) 1054cc44 [#9047]: Fixed horizontal scrollbar in chat by adding overflow-x-hidden to MessageList ScrollArea. (3) 055aeeab [#9048]: Fixed PlanApprovalBar never showing - removed !isStreaming guard since plan_pending_approval fires while agent is blocked on ExitPlanMode gate (isStreaming stays true until after approval).

PlanApprovalBar timing bug: plan_pending_approval WebSocket event fires while the agent is blocked on the ExitPlanMode gate, but isStreaming stays true until the chat_stream done message arrives (which only happens AFTER the plan is approved and execution continues). So !isStreaming guards on PlanApprovalBar rendering will prevent it from ever showing. The planPendingApproval state alone is sufficient — it's only set to true when the backend explicitly signals the agent is blocked.

ChatInput button layout fix (ChatInput.tsx): Replaced conditional streaming/non-streaming button blocks with a single stable container using 'flex gap-1 shrink-0'. Send button is always present (left), disabled when not streaming and no input. Stop button appears conditionally to the right during streaming. This prevents the bar from growing vertically when buttons appear/disappear. Send stays enabled during streaming to allow interrupt-with-message.

Web chat ScrollArea component (web/src/components/chat/ui/ScrollArea.tsx) only sets overflow-y-auto by default. MessageList needs overflow-x-hidden explicitly to prevent horizontal scrollbar from wide content like tables or code blocks. Fixed in MessageList.tsx by adding the class to the ScrollArea usage.

Web chat UI artifact system: Plans should use createArtifact('text', ...) not 'code' so they render as markdown in ArtifactTextView. ArtifactTextView supports an optional onChange prop that switches from Source/Preview toggle to Edit/View toggle with CodeMirror editor. ArtifactPanel accepts plan approval props (planPendingApproval, onApprovePlan, onRequestPlanChanges, isStreaming) to render PlanApprovalBar inline.

Session #1742 got stuck in plan mode due to the artifact panel approval bug. ExitPlanMode was called but user couldn't approve because ArtifactPanel doesn't render PlanApprovalBar. The UserPromptSubmit hook keeps re-injecting plan-mode status=active, blocking all writes. Another agent was dispatched to fix the artifact panel approval UI separately.

Web UI known bugs (as of 2026-02-23): All three original bugs are now FIXED. Bug 1 & 2 (ChatInput send/stop button order and vertical expansion) fixed via ChatInput button layout refactor. Bug 3 (PlanApprovalBar not rendered in ArtifactPanel) fixed in commit ab65468e [#9046] — PlanApprovalBar moved into ArtifactPanel, plan artifacts changed to type 'text', ArtifactTextView got Edit/View toggle with CodeMirror.

Web chat usage persistence pipeline architecture: ResultMessage.usage (optional dict) flows through chat_session.py (token extraction + context_window resolution via litellm/model fallback) → DoneEvent dataclass → chat.py _stream_chat_response (persistence to DB). Two persistence paths in chat.py: (1) has_usage=True → session_manager.update_usage() with full token data + context_window + model, (2) has_usage=False → session_manager.db.safe_update() for just context_window + model. The update_usage method in storage/sessions.py uses COALESCE for context_window and model to never overwrite good data with NULL. Other callers of update_usage: admin.py (API endpoint) and sessions/lifecycle.py (transcript processing).

Web chat context pie chart fix (#9045): The `0 or None` pattern in Python converts 0 to None (falsy), which broke the usage persistence pipeline. When SDK ResultMessage.usage is missing (None), token counts default to 0, then `0 or None` → None, then the persistence guard skips update_usage entirely. Fix: use a `has_usage = isinstance(_raw_usage, dict)` flag to distinguish 'usage reported as 0' from 'usage not reported'. Also: always persist context_window + model to DB even without token data, via a safe_update fallback in chat.py. The `update_usage` method in storage/sessions.py now accepts an optional `model` parameter with COALESCE.

LLM model routing map (as of 2026-02-23): Haiku is used for: session title synthesis (SessionTitleConfig), memory extraction (MemoryExtractionConfig), skill description (SkillDescriptionConfig), digest generation (DigestConfig), tool summarization, task description, MCP server import, memory fact/dedup/entity extraction. Sonnet is used for: merge conflict resolution (MergeResolutionConfig), session summary, tool recommendations. Opus is used for: code review (ReviewConfig), chat (ChatConfig). All are configurable via DaemonConfig fields.

Pattern for config-driven LLM callsites: Create a config class (e.g., MemoryExtractionConfig) with provider/model fields in config/features.py or config/sessions.py, wire into DaemonConfig in config/app.py, then at the callsite use get_provider_for_feature(config) instead of get_default_provider(). Always wrap in try/except falling back to get_default_provider(). When passing model to generate_text(), use **gen_kwargs pattern to avoid passing model=None (tests assert exact call signatures).

Backend auth system (commit 009b1690 from 2026-02-23). Files: src/gobby/servers/routes/auth.py (login/logout/status endpoints), src/gobby/servers/middleware/auth.py (AuthMiddleware), src/gobby/storage/auth.py (AuthStore — originally PBKDF2-SHA256 but now uses Fernet encryption via SecretStore). Config model AuthConfig in src/gobby/config/app.py has username, password, session_secret fields. session_secret is dead code — sessions use random DB-backed tokens, not signed cookies. Password stored via config_key_to_secret_name('auth.password') in secrets table. CLI: `gobby auth` (--remove to disable). Frontend: useAuth hook + LoginPage component with auth guard in App.tsx. Middleware lets '/' through unauthenticated.

Task #8906 (Add sources field to skill audience filtering) has a complete implementation plan at .gobby/plans/8906-sources-field.md. Plan was approved. TDD approach: 6 tests across test_parser.py and test_injector.py, changes to parser.py (SkillAudienceConfig dataclass + frontmatter extraction + to_dict) and injector.py (_matches_audience source check). Follows exact same pattern as existing task_categories field.

UX convention: All text inputs in the web UI should use Shift+Enter for newline and Enter for submit. This applies to AskUserQuestion 'Other' input, PlanApprovalBar feedback textarea, and any future text inputs. Josh confirmed this is the expected behavior pattern.

Bug fix verified: ToolCallCard.tsx AskUserQuestion 'Other' text input now has onKeyDown handler for Enter key submission (lines 402-407). Previously the input had no key handler and required clicking the Submit button. Fix uses Enter (no shift) to submit, matching standard form behavior.

Bug fix verified: The spurious plan approval bar trigger (setPlanPendingApproval on chunk.done in plan mode) has been removed from useChat.ts. Plan approval now only fires from the plan_pending_approval WebSocket event sent by ExitPlanMode. The old code at ~line 406-408 that auto-triggered on any streaming completion in plan mode is gone.

skill-scanner (Cisco, PyPI) real API: `run_scan()` in `skill_scanner.analyzers.pipeline` requires LLM provider or VirusTotal API key. For local no-API-key scanning, use `run_static_rules()` from `skill_scanner.validation.static_rules` which does regex-based detection (prompt injection, exfiltration, obfuscated commands, filesystem attacks, credential harvesting). Risk scoring via `evaluate_risk()` in `skill_scanner.scoring.risk`. Models in `skill_scanner.models.{targets,findings,reports}`. The original scanner.py was written against a hallucinated API (StaticAnalyzer, BehavioralAnalyzer classes that don't exist).

The plugin system has been removed from Gobby. The PluginsCard was deleted from the Dashboard UI in #9042. The /admin/status endpoint does not return a plugins field. Any remaining plugin references in cli/extensions.py are legacy code.

Web UI delete confirmation pattern: All destructive actions use window.confirm() before executing. Examples: RulesTab.tsx:87, McpPage.tsx:71, MemoryDetail.tsx:113, AgentsTab.tsx:381, PipelinesTab.tsx:94, TerminalsPage.tsx:122, ConfigurationPage.tsx:418. SkillsPage.tsx was missing this and was fixed in #9041.

Session #1749 summary: Implemented 5 fixes from plan (commit 517cc2f3) plus a follow-up correction (commit e1606a88). Closed 8 tasks total: #9026 (plan not shown), #9027 (no artifact), #9028 (ExitPlanMode gate), #9029 (sentence collision), #9030 (web SDK auto-approve), #9031 (task-close loop), #9037 (umbrella), #9040 (template fix). Key files modified: chat_session_permissions.py (ExitPlanMode blocking gate), websocket/chat.py (has_sent_text + plan_ready callback), session_control.py (plan approval routing), useChat.ts (plan_pending_approval handler), ChatPage.tsx (artifact integration), require-task-close.yaml (when guard + template), sessions.py (title synthesis with haiku), all LLM providers (max_tokens param).

task_ref is a session variable set by claim_task (_lifecycle.py:624) and create_task (_crud.py:175) in '#N' format (e.g. '#9037'). It's initialized to empty string by init-task-ref.yaml rule and cleared back to '' by close_task (_lifecycle.py:272). It's available in Jinja templates in rule definitions because _build_eval_context() (rule_engine.py:272-275) flattens all session variables to top-level context keys. Don't use claimed_task_id (UUID) for display — use task_ref.

Pattern: storage.get_memory() (in storage/memories.py) raises ValueError when a memory doesn't exist — it does NOT return None. When iterating over memory IDs from external indices (Qdrant, crossrefs, etc.), always wrap in try/except ValueError to handle stale references gracefully.

Bug fix: storage.get_memory() raises ValueError for missing memories, but manager.py search paths (lines 541, 571) and crossref lookup (line 899) expected it to return None. Fixed by wrapping calls in try/except ValueError to gracefully skip deleted/missing memories referenced by stale search indices.

require-task-close rule fix: The stop-gate rule at install/shared/rules/stop-gates/require-task-close.yaml had two bugs. (1) The Jinja template used {{ task_ref }} which is never set in the rule engine's eval context — only in commit_policy.py's context. Fixed to use {{ claimed_task_id[:8] if claimed_task_id else '(unknown)' }} which is available via _build_eval_context() flattening workflow variables. (2) The when condition didn't guard against empty claimed_task_id, causing the rule to fire with blank task IDs. Added `and variables.get('claimed_task_id')` guard.

LLM generate_text interface now supports max_tokens parameter across all providers (base.py, claude.py, gemini.py, litellm.py, codex.py). SDK path in claude.py post-truncates since ClaudeAgentOptions doesn't support max_tokens. LiteLLM paths pass max_tokens directly to acompletion. Default is 8000 when not specified. Session title synthesis uses model='haiku' with max_tokens=30 to prevent unbounded output.

Sentence collision bug root cause: In websocket/chat.py, the TextChunk handler checked `if accumulated_text and not accumulated_text.endswith(...)` to inject a \n\n separator after tool calls. But accumulated_text was already flushed to '' by the ToolCallEvent handler, making the condition always False. Fix: added a `has_sent_text` boolean flag (initialized False, set True when non-empty text is sent) that survives the flush. The separator check now uses `if has_sent_text` instead of checking accumulated_text.

ExitPlanMode approval gate pattern: In chat_session_permissions.py, ExitPlanMode now uses the same asyncio.Event blocking pattern as AskUserQuestion. It reads the plan file, broadcasts plan_pending_approval to the frontend, then blocks on _pending_plan_event.wait() with 600s timeout. The frontend shows the approval bar and opens the plan in the artifact panel. User decision routes through session_control.py → provide_plan_decision() which sets _pending_plan_decision and unblocks the event. Approve → set_chat_mode('accept_edits') + PermissionResultAllow. Reject → PermissionResultDeny with feedback so agent stays in plan mode.

Session title synthesis: Updated to use PromptLoader system (sessions/synthesize_title prompt), model='haiku' instead of default opus, max_tokens=30. Post-truncation safety net: first line only, strip quotes, cap at 100 chars. Manual rename validates title <= 200 chars (400 error). New prompt file at src/gobby/install/shared/prompts/sessions/synthesize_title.md.

LLM generate_text max_tokens parameter: Added max_tokens parameter to LLMProvider.generate_text() abstract interface and all 4 implementations (claude, gemini, litellm, codex). For Claude SDK path, max_tokens can't be passed to ClaudeAgentOptions, so post-truncation is used (result[:max_tokens*4]). For LiteLLM/OpenAI paths, it's passed directly to acompletion/create. Default remains 8000 when not specified.

Sentence collision fix in websocket/chat.py: The has_sent_text flag tracks whether any text was sent in the current turn, independent of accumulated_text flushes. The old check `if accumulated_text and not accumulated_text.endswith(...)` was always False because accumulated_text was already flushed to '' by ToolCallEvent handler before the separator check ran. The fix uses `has_sent_text` which survives the flush.

ExitPlanMode approval gate pattern: In chat_session_permissions.py, ExitPlanMode now blocks using asyncio.Event (same pattern as AskUserQuestion) until user approves or requests changes via provide_plan_decision(). The backend sends a 'plan_pending_approval' WS message to the frontend with plan_content read from _plan_file_path. On approve, it sets mode to accept_edits and broadcasts mode_changed with reason 'plan_approved'. On request_changes, it denies the tool with feedback so agent stays in plan mode. The frontend handles this in useChat.ts with a dedicated handler that sets planPendingApproval=true and triggers artifact creation via onPlanReadyRef callback.

Session #1748: Implemented Drawbridge UI tasks. (1) Removed WHEN/EFFECT code blocks from RulesTab.tsx - deleted RuleCodeBlock, codeTheme, syntax-highlighter imports, expand state/props. YAML button remains as sole detail viewer. (2) Font consistency across 4 CSS files: converted hardcoded px to calc(var(--font-size-base) * factor). Scale: titles=1.0 (16px), counts=1.25 (20px), names=0.875 (14px), descriptions/inputs=0.75 (12px), badges=0.625 (10px). Removed var(--font-mono) from skills-row-name, skills-detail-title, agent form inputs (monospace only for code/IDs). Commit: 72fdfe97.

Rules cards no longer show WHEN/EFFECT/MATCH code blocks on expand. The RuleCodeBlock component and react-syntax-highlighter dependency were removed from RulesTab.tsx. The YAML editor modal (via the YAML button) is the sole way to view/edit rule details. The expanded state, detail loading, and fetchRuleDetail-on-expand logic were all removed.

Web UI font consistency: admin pages (Agents, Skills, MCP Servers, Rules/Workflows) now use calc(var(--font-size-base) * factor) instead of hardcoded px values. The scale: 1.0 for page titles (16px), 1.25 for overview counts (20px), 0.875 for item names (14px), 0.75 for descriptions/inputs/buttons (12px), 0.625 for badges/labels (10px). Chat page uses Tailwind text-sm (14px) for content and text-xs (12px) for metadata. Skills row names and detail titles no longer use var(--font-mono) — monospace is reserved for code/IDs only (e.g., .mcp-tool-name, .mcp-detail-header h3).

AppErrorBoundary exists in web/src/App.tsx wrapping all tab content. It accepts activeTab and onReturnToChat props. It auto-resets error state when the user switches tabs (componentDidUpdate checks prevProps.activeTab). Shows error message, 'Try Again' button (resets error state), and 'Return to Chat' button. This prevents any tab crash from unmounting the entire React tree — the sidebar and shell remain intact.

Web UI code splitting pattern: All non-default page components in App.tsx are lazy-loaded with React.lazy() and wrapped in Suspense inside the AppErrorBoundary. Vendor deps are split via manualChunks in vite.config.ts: codemirror, xterm, syntax-highlighter, markdown, d3. MemoryGraph (2D) and KnowledgeGraph (3D) are both lazy-loaded in MemoryPage.tsx. The lazy import pattern is: const Page = lazy(() => import('./components/Page').then(m => ({ default: m.Page }))). ChatPage stays eagerly loaded as the default tab.

React error boundaries only catch synchronous render/lifecycle errors. Async errors from Three.js/WebGL (in requestAnimationFrame loops) escape them entirely. In React 18, unhandled errors cause the entire root tree to unmount and re-mount with default state. Fix pattern: (1) App-level ErrorBoundary wrapping tab content for synchronous crash recovery, (2) window.addEventListener('error'/'unhandledrejection') with keyword filtering for async WebGL/Three.js errors, (3) canvas.addEventListener('webglcontextlost') for GPU context loss. Use refs for callbacks in window listeners to avoid stale closures without re-registering listeners.

Three plan-mode bugs filed: #9026 (P1) plan mode exits without showing plan content to user, #9027 (P2) plans never written to artifact/preview window, #9028 (P1) ExitPlanMode doesn't require actual user approval — auto-succeeds with no user interaction gate. The entire plan-mode review flow is currently non-functional: agent writes file nobody sees, 'requests approval' that auto-succeeds, starts coding. Fix likely in hooks system — ExitPlanMode callback needs to render plan in UI and block on user confirmation.

React 18 error boundary limitation: Error boundaries only catch errors during rendering, lifecycle methods, and constructors. They do NOT catch errors in event handlers, async code (setTimeout, requestAnimationFrame, promises). Three.js render loops use requestAnimationFrame, so WebGL crashes bypass error boundaries entirely. When no error boundary catches a render error in React 18, the ENTIRE root tree is unmounted (different from React 17 which left corrupted tree mounted).

Memory tab crash root cause: When Neo4j is configured, viewMode auto-switches to 'knowledge' which lazy-loads KnowledgeGraph.tsx (Three.js/WebGL). With 955 entities + 2238 relationships, ForceGraph3D creates 955 SpriteText objects (each with canvas texture). Three.js crashes happen in requestAnimationFrame (async), which React error boundaries CANNOT catch. No global ErrorBoundary in App.tsx means React 18 unmounts the entire root tree, and activeTab resets to default 'chat'. Fix: add App-level ErrorBoundary, lower DEFAULT_KNOWLEDGE_GRAPH_LIMIT from 5000→500, add WebGL context-lost handlers in KnowledgeGraph.tsx.

React useCallback stale closure pattern in Gobby web UI: when an async handler inside useCallback references state (like graphData) and also appears in the dependency array, two problems occur: (1) the callback recreates on every state change causing unnecessary re-renders, and (2) if the async operation completes after state changes, it merges with stale data. Fix: use functional state updates — setGraphData(prev => prev ? mergeGraphData(prev, data) : data) — which removes the state from the dep array and always uses current state. Applied in KnowledgeGraph.tsx handleNodeClick.

KnowledgeGraph.tsx crash: fg.d3Force('charge').strength(-120) crashes when d3Force() returns undefined (force simulation not yet initialized). The 2D MemoryGraph already had optional chaining on these calls but the 3D KnowledgeGraph did not. Error occurs in useEffect so the KnowledgeGraphErrorBoundary doesn't catch it. Fix: add ?. to all three d3Force() calls at line 189. Also fixed stale graphData closure in handleNodeClick by using functional setGraphData(prev => ...) instead of capturing graphData in the dependency array.

The full Agent Definition Migration was completed across 3 sessions with 6 commits:
1. 16b94f1f - Rename 'generic' agent to 'default' (#8974)
2. 2d045d74 - Evolve AgentDefinitionBody schema with AgentWorkflows (#8982)
3. d24fb489 - Consolidate spawn_agent factory and delete _v2.py (#9023)
4. 0221d236 - Fresh agent YAMLs, simplify sync (#9023)
5. 4ccee279 - Delete AgentDefinition model and all conversion code (#9023)
6. 1df11664 - Add agent definition CRUD tools to gobby-agents MCP server (#9024)

Key architecture: AgentDefinitionBody (15 fields) is now the single source of truth. Old AgentDefinition (29 fields), WorkflowSpec, AgentDefinitionLoader all deleted. Agent defs stored in workflow_definitions table with workflow_type='agent'. Bundled YAML sync parses directly as AgentDefinitionBody. MCP tools for CRUD in agent_definitions.py.

Agent Definition Architecture (post-migration, Feb 2026):

The old AgentDefinition model (29 fields, in agents/definitions.py) has been DELETED along with WorkflowSpec, SkillProfileConfig, AgentDefinitionLoader, AgentDefinitionInfo, and AgentSource.

The single source of truth is now AgentDefinitionBody (15 fields, in workflows/definitions.py) stored in workflow_definitions table with workflow_type='agent'. Key schema:
- AgentDefinitionBody: name, description, role, goal, personality, instructions, provider, model, mode, isolation, base_branch, timeout, max_turns, workflows (AgentWorkflows), enabled
- AgentWorkflows: pipeline (str|None), rules (list[str]), variables (dict)

Loading pattern: Use LocalWorkflowDefinitionManager.list_all(workflow_type='agent') then AgentDefinitionBody.model_validate_json(row.definition_json). No intermediate conversion.

Key code locations:
- Model: src/gobby/workflows/definitions.py (AgentDefinitionBody, AgentWorkflows)
- Sync: src/gobby/agents/sync.py (YAML → DB sync at startup)
- Factory: src/gobby/mcp_proxy/tools/spawn_agent/_factory.py (_load_agent_body)
- Routes: src/gobby/servers/routes/agents.py (CRUD + import/export)
- Bundled agents: src/gobby/install/shared/agents/ (5 YAML files)
- Old agents: src/gobby/install/shared/agents/deprecated/

Runner (agents/runner.py) no longer loads agent definitions — the factory handles all definition merging before spawn.

E2E test project ID leakage fix (commit 5842ff7c): Three root causes identified. (1) find_free_port() in tests/e2e/conftest.py was binding to localhost:0, which on macOS with SO_REUSEADDR could coexist with production daemon on 0.0.0.0 — fixed by binding to 0.0.0.0 and restricting to port range 30000-40000. (2) get_project_context() in src/gobby/utils/project_context.py unconditionally checked the contextvars.ContextVar before filesystem, even when explicit cwd was passed — fixed by gating the check on cwd being None. (3) No guard against test project IDs in production — added set_project_context() guard blocking 'e2e-test-project' and 'test-project' IDs unless GOBBY_TEST_PROTECT env var is set.

Bug: MCP proxy get_current_session resolved project_id to 'e2e-test-project' instead of the actual project. Session #1733 was not found in DB, task creation appeared to succeed (#9019) but the task was unfindable, and all FK-dependent MCP calls failed. Likely a test fixture or test DB leaking into the running daemon's project context.

Pre-existing issue: 14 DB-based tests in tests/workflows/test_task_enforcement_rules.py fail because _sync_bundled() + manager.get_by_name() returns None for all task-enforcement rules (block-native-task-tools, require-commit-before-close, block-skip-validation-with-commit, block-ask-during-stop-compliance, track-task-claim, track-task-release, and require-task-before-edit). The sync function doesn't find rules from the split file structure under task-enforcement/. Only condition evaluation tests (which don't use DB sync) pass.

The require-task-before-edit rule's plan file exemption was fixed in commit fd0dd823. Previously it required both plan_mode variable AND is_plan_file() to exempt plan files, but plan_mode depended on detect-plan-mode-enter/exit rules being enabled. Now is_plan_file() alone exempts plan files unconditionally. The is_plan_file() function (in gobby.workflows.enforcement.blocking) checks for .md files in .gobby/plans/ or .claude/plans/ directories.

Terminal context cleanup (for future work): Since tmux is the only terminal used, these can be removed from get_terminal_context() in hook_dispatcher.py: term_session_id (Terminal.app), iterm_session_id (iTerm2), kitty_window_id (Kitty), alacritty_socket (Alacritty), vscode_terminal_id (VS Code). Also clean up: TERMINAL_CONTEXT_KEYS in event_enrichment.py, terminal key loops in adapters (claude_code.py, gemini.py, codex_impl/adapter.py), and kill strategies in agents/registry.py (Kitty, Alacritty, iTerm strategies).

Tasks table dead weight analysis (for future cleanup):
- DEAD columns safe to drop: verification (set but never read), is_expanded (redundant with expansion_status), original_instruction (orphaned - in schema but not dataclass), details (orphaned), estimated_subtasks (written but never read)
- ALIVE columns that look dead but aren't: compacted_at/summary (used by compaction), expansion_context (used by expansion workflow), complexity_score (used in task selection scoring)
- Dead tables: session_memories (in schema but no code references), passkey_credentials/auth_sessions/auth_challenges (only in live DB, not in code)

Bug (FIXED in commit fd0dd823): require-task-before-edit rule previously blocked plan file writes because its condition depended on `plan_mode` variable (set by detect-plan-mode-enter/exit rules), but those rules are independently enabled and often disabled. Fix: removed the `plan_mode` guard so `is_plan_file()` is always evaluated; plan files are now always exempt from task-before-edit. File: src/gobby/install/shared/rules/task-enforcement/require-task-before-edit.yaml line 9.

SQLite column removal pattern used in migration v121: Since SQLite doesn't support DROP COLUMN on older versions, use table recreation: (1) fetchall existing rows, (2) DROP TABLE, (3) CREATE TABLE with new schema (without removed columns), (4) INSERT rows back selecting only kept columns. This pattern preserves data and FK constraints. Used to remove observations/reflection_pending from workflow_states.

Bug: gobby-memory search_memories can fail with 'Memory X not found' error when a deleted/stale memory ID is still referenced internally (possibly in cross-references or embeddings index). Observed when searching for 'observations reflection WorkflowState' — returned error referencing memory 55ce6532-9ac3-5f1b-9d1a-b1580ef125bc which no longer exists. Workaround: create a new memory instead of updating the stale one.

Observations system uses session variables, not WorkflowState fields. The `observe` rule effect (added in commit b2a3c375) appends structured entries to `_observations` session variable via RuleEngine. Context injection reads observations from SessionVariableManager.get_variables(session_id).get('_observations', []). Dead fields `observations` and `reflection_pending` were removed from WorkflowState model, state_manager SQL, and all callsites. Migration v121 drops the columns from workflow_states table.

Observations system: WorkflowState no longer has observations/reflection_pending fields (removed in migration v121). Observations are now stored in the _observations session variable via SessionVariableManager. The 'observe' rule effect (5th type after block/set_variable/inject_context/mcp_call) appends structured entries {category, message, timestamp, rule} to _observations in the variables dict during rule evaluation. Context injection reads observations from session_variable_manager.get_variables(session_id).get('_observations', []). The handle_inject_context wrapper creates SessionVariableManager(context.db) and passes it to inject_context.

All CLI adapters (Claude Code, Gemini, Cursor, Windsurf, Copilot, Codex) nest the Bash command inside event.data.tool_input.command, NOT at event.data.command. Any code in rule_engine.py that reads command from event.data must also check tool_input as fallback. Fixed in _should_block() (commit 80a52a8a).

tests/workflows/test_task_enforcement_rules.py has 16 pre-existing failures: _sync_bundled(db) + manager.get_by_name() returns None for all task-enforcement rule names (block-native-task-tools, require-task-before-edit, etc.). The bundled rule sync mechanism changed but these tests weren't updated. Tracked as #9011.

is_plan_file() is a condition helper for the rule engine, defined in src/gobby/workflows/enforcement/blocking.py. It checks if a file path is in .gobby/plans/ or ~/.claude/plans/ and ends with .md. Registered as an allowed_func in rule_engine.py _evaluate_condition(). Used by require-task-before-edit rule to unconditionally exempt plan file edits without a claimed task (no longer requires plan_mode variable after commit fd0dd823).

- test_event_handlers.py is at tests/hooks/ not tests/workflows/ (all 115 pass)
- test_handler_reuse now passes (was #8954)

When sessions crash during workflow engine refactoring, new sessions often pick up the same work and commit under new task IDs, leaving original tasks stale in in_progress state. To triage: cross-reference git log task ID tags against open task list, run the actual tests mentioned in bug tasks to check if issues still reproduce, then close as already_implemented (with commit_sha from the actual fix commit) or duplicate. close_task with reason='already_implemented' or 'duplicate' auto-skips validation but still needs commit_sha if the original session had edits.

Pre-existing test failures found 2026-02-23: (1) test_toggle_rule_rejects_template in test_rule_engine.py - toggle_rule incorrectly returns success=True for template-only rules (filed #9009). (2) 20 failures in test_progressive_disclosure_rules.py - rule lookups return None and integration tests fail to block, likely caused by removed/renamed progressive disclosure rule templates (filed #9010).

RuleEngine._evaluate_condition (rule_engine.py) now uses build_condition_helpers() from safe_evaluator.py as the canonical source of allowed_funcs, with rule-engine-specific helpers (is_server_listed, is_tool_unlocked, is_discovery_tool, isinstance) layered on top. Any new helpers added to build_condition_helpers() will automatically be available in rule conditions.

ConversationPicker component (Chats sidebar) is rendered in ChatPage.tsx, which receives a ConversationState prop from App.tsx. To add new props: update ConversationPickerProps interface, ConversationState in types/chat.ts, the conversations object in App.tsx (~line 422), and the prop pass-through in ChatPage.tsx (~line 46).

TasksPage.tsx uses both localStats (computed from client-side max 200 tasks) and server-side stats from useTasks(). The TaskOverview component should use server-side stats for correct global counts. The localStats is still used in the filter chips bar to show counts of visible tasks. Default task filter should be 'open' (useTasks.ts:134) to avoid closed tasks dominating the view.

Web UI CSS architecture: Chat sidebar uses var(--bg-tertiary) for background and calc(var(--font-size-base) * 0.85) for title font-size. Terminals sidebar should match these values. The conversation-picker styles are at index.css:1869+, terminals-sidebar styles at index.css:2897+. Agent card styles are at index.css:11355+.

The deprecated session-lifecycle.yaml workflow used action-based enforcement (block_tools, track_schema_lookup, track_discovery_step actions registered in actions.py). These were removed in #8980. The new rule engine uses standalone YAML rule files with effects (type: block) evaluated directly in rule_engine.py. Key functions kept in blocking.py: DISCOVERY_TOOLS, is_discovery_tool(), is_tool_unlocked(), is_server_listed(), is_plan_file(). The rule engine supports command_pattern/command_not_pattern natively (rule_engine.py:309-327). Note: is_plan_file() was added to the rule engine's allowed_funcs as part of #9003.

hook_manager.handle() execution order: For SESSION_START events, the handler must run BEFORE workflow rules evaluate. This is because the session handler registers the session and sets event.metadata['_platform_session_id']. Without this, WorkflowHookHandler._evaluate_rules() falls back to event.session_id (the CLI's external_id) at hooks.py:78, causing set_variable effects to be stored under the wrong key. For all other events, rules evaluate first so block effects can prevent handler execution. Fixed in commit dcb6af09.

WorkflowHookHandler.handle() now delegates directly to evaluate() (rule engine). The step-based workflow engine (engine.handle_event) is dead code — 0 workflows in DB use it. Before this fix, handle() ran evaluate() then ran the dead engine and returned its empty response, discarding rule metadata including mcp_call effects. This caused title synthesis and other mcp_call-based features to silently fail.

Event handlers (_agent.py, _tool.py, _session.py) no longer call _evaluate_workflows() — rules are evaluated once in hook_manager.handle() via workflow_handler.handle(), not twice. The _apply_debug_echo() method no longer takes a wf_response parameter; it reads debug_echo_context from WorkflowConfig only.

Hook system architecture: Rules evaluate ONCE centrally in hook_manager.handle() via workflow_handler.handle() -> evaluate(). Event handlers (_agent.py, _tool.py, _session.py) do NOT evaluate rules themselves. Before #8994 fix, rules were evaluated twice (once in hook_manager, once in each handler via _evaluate_workflows), and the central path discarded rule metadata (including mcp_calls) by running the dead step-based engine after rule evaluation. The fix made handle() return evaluate() directly, preserving mcp_call metadata for dispatch. The _evaluate_workflows method was removed from EventHandlersBase.

Removed dead code fields from workflow definitions: WorkflowStep.check_rules (list[str]) and WorkflowDefinition.on_error (list[dict[str, Any]]). Neither field was ever read by production code. check_rules was declared at definitions.py:221 and on_error at definitions.py:276. The step workflow engine (engine.py + unified_evaluator.py) is legacy code slated for removal per docs/plans/workflows-v2.md Phase 6. step.rules IS referenced in unified_evaluator.py:203 (separate from check_rules). step.on_mcp_error IS used by engine.py:641 (separate from on_error). Also cleaned up deprecated/developer.yaml which had check_rules entries.

DevMode Install button: All 3 workflow tabs (PipelinesTab, AgentsTab, RulesTab) now show an Install button in the devMode branch for template cards, matching the non-devMode behavior. Previously devMode hid the Install button, leaving only YAML/Edit/Duplicate/Delete actions.

Sync template restoration policy: sync_bundled_rules and sync_bundled_workflows now restore soft-deleted templates instead of skipping them. Templates should always be available — if users don't want one, they disable it. Restored templates are set to enabled=False. Also: when an installed copy shadows a template and the template row is missing or soft-deleted, the sync now creates/restores the template row (previously it silently skipped). The template row query was widened to include soft-deleted rows (removed `AND deleted_at IS NULL`).

WorkflowsPage tab architecture: All 3 tabs (Pipelines, Agents, Rules) are now extracted into consistent tab components: PipelinesTab.tsx, AgentsTab.tsx, RulesTab.tsx. WorkflowsPage.tsx is a thin shell (~170 lines) with toolbar, TabBar, and tab routing. YamlEditorModal is exported from WorkflowsPage and shared by all tabs. Each tab receives searchText, sourceFilter, devMode, refreshKey props. Create buttons stay in the toolbar; create UI (forms/modals/dropdowns) renders inside each tab via showCreate* props.

When using get_by_name for rules/workflows that may have both template and installed copies with the same name, use the fallback pattern: `get_by_name(name) or get_by_name(name, include_templates=True)`. This prefers installed copies (which the user interacts with) and only falls back to templates when no installed copy exists. Do NOT use include_templates=True directly as it may return the template instead of the installed copy, breaking toggle/YAML operations.

The require-uv hook rule is producing false positives - it blocks ALL Bash commands including those that already use 'uv run'. This was encountered in session #1711 and prevented running tests. The recent commit f21ed616 (bare variable false positives fix) may need a daemon restart to take effect, or there may be an additional bug in the rule.

Renamed use_as_template → install_from_template and use_all_bundled_as_templates → install_all_templates across the entire codebase. Routes changed: /use-as-template → /install, /use-all-bundled-as-templates → /install-all-templates. Also fixed a bug where install_from_template hardcoded enabled=True instead of preserving original.enabled. Frontend components (RulesTab, WorkflowsPage, AgentDefinitionsPage) and hooks (useRules, useWorkflows) were all updated. Button labels changed from 'Use as Template' to 'Install'.

RuleEffect.mcp_tools field is list[str] with format 'server:tool' (e.g., 'gobby-tasks:close_task') or 'server:*' for wildcards. It is NOT a list of dicts. The rule engine matches mcp_tools against event.data['mcp_tool'] and event.data['mcp_server']. For call_tool events, _build_eval_context() unwraps the nested arguments dict so rule conditions like tool_input.get('commit_sha') resolve against inner tool params.

Rule engine block messages now include the rule name. In rule_engine.py line 119, after Jinja rendering, block_reason is prepended with 'Rule enforced by Gobby: [{_row.name}]\n'. This means all hook error messages from rule blocks show which rule fired. The change is in the single code path where block effects are applied (the evaluate() method's block handling loop).

Confirmed dead code in workflow definitions (session #1699 research): (1) WorkflowState.observations - REMOVED in migration v121 (was initialized to [] but never appended to; now reimplemented as _observations session variable), (2) WorkflowState.reflection_pending - REMOVED in migration v121 (was always False, never set True; now reimplemented via observe rule effect), (3) WorkflowStep.check_rules - field declared but no code reads it (step.rules IS used, check_rules is separate dead field), (4) WorkflowStep.on_error - declared but engine uses on_mcp_error instead. NOT dead (research agents were wrong): session_variables on WorkflowDefinition, task_list/current_task_index/files_modified_this_task, exit_when/exit_conditions, on_premature_stop, step.rules.

Agent definition redesign vision (task #8982): Restructure AgentDefinition.workflows from dict[str, WorkflowSpec] to a flat struct with: workflows.pipeline (one pipeline name, DB-backed not file), workflows.rules (list of rule names), workflows.variables (pre-seed session vars, override rule defaults). Pipeline auto-start is configurable. YAML files are templates, DB is source of truth. Agents become orchestration containers. Depends on generic→default rename (#8974) and variable layer cleanup.

Variable architecture has 2 active scopes: (1) WorkflowInstance.variables (per-workflow per-session, in workflow_instances table), (2) SessionVariableManager (shared across all workflows, in session_variables table, primary/preferred). WorkflowState.variables is legacy and no longer used as a fallback (removed in v0.2.22). Rules use SessionVariableManager exclusively via set_variable effects. Pipelines have their own separate input/output data flow and don't use workflow state variables at all.

task_ref workflow variable: Set to `#{seq_num}` (e.g., `#8978`) when claiming/creating a task, cleared to `""` when closing. Previously the require-task-close rule template used `{{ task_ref }}` which rendered empty when task_ref wasn't initialized (not a missing-from-context issue — task_ref IS available via _build_eval_context() flattening session variables). Template was changed to `{{ claimed_task_id[:8] if claimed_task_id else '(unknown)' }}`. task_ref is also used in commit_policy.py context. Must be initialized to `""` via init-task-ref.yaml to prevent empty rendering. Similarly, task_claimed must be initialized to `false` via init-task-claimed.yaml to prevent NameError in the `when` condition.

Rule engine `when` conditions that use bare variable names (e.g., `task_claimed`) instead of `variables.get('task_claimed')` will raise NameError via SafeExpressionEvaluator when the variable is undefined. For `block` effects, this fails closed (always blocks). All rule variables used in bare form must have corresponding init rules in `session-defaults/` that set defaults on `session_start`. The `installed` source in workflow_definitions is a copy of a `template` row — when sync.py updates a template's definition_json, it must also propagate to the installed copy via `_propagate_to_installed()`.

Rule engine mcp_call effects are dispatched by HookManager._dispatch_mcp_calls() (added in ddc4aa7c). The rule engine builds mcp_calls into HookResponse.metadata['mcp_calls'] but does NOT dispatch them itself — HookManager.handle() extracts them after workflow evaluation and dispatches via ToolProxyService. Context injection: session_id comes from event.metadata['_platform_session_id'], prompt_text from event.data['prompt']. Background calls use asyncio fire-and-forget pattern matching broadcasting.

Web UI project selection architecture: The app-level project selector in App.tsx provides `effectiveProjectId` which should be passed as a prop to all tab pages that need project scoping. Pages should NOT manage their own projectId state — this causes the backend `_resolve_project` to fall back to the first project alphabetically. The pattern is: App.tsx passes `projectId={effectiveProjectId}` to page components, which forward it to their data hooks (e.g., `useSourceControl(projectId)`). Fixed in commit 3b459a62 for GitHubPage.

User preference: workflow filter UI should use a dropdown/select element, not chips/buttons. Chips take up too much space. The dropdown should match the height of the search box (same padding, font-size, border styles).

Bundled rules split from 11 group YAML files into ~80 individual files in group subdirectories under src/gobby/install/shared/rules/<group-name>/. Old group files moved to deprecated/. Individual files use tags: [group-name] instead of group: field. sync_bundled_rules() skips deprecated/ via path filter and has orphan cleanup for removed rules.

Source values renamed in workflow_definitions table: 'bundled' → 'template', 'custom' → 'installed'. Migration 120 handles existing DB rows. Backward-compat SQL uses IN ('bundled', 'template') in sync.py for transition period. Prompts scope='bundled' is a different concept and was NOT renamed. Skills 'bundled' stat also left unchanged.

Rules in src/gobby/install/shared/rules/ are templates with enabled: false by default. They get synced/installed to the database where they're actually enabled. Don't modify the install folder to enable rules — they're already installed and enabled in the DB.

Don't use skip_validation on close_task unless there's a genuine reason (e.g. validator can't see the diff, truncated context). Tasks without validation_criteria just need a linked commit_sha to close normally. Using skip_validation unnecessarily routes tasks to needs_review instead of closing them.
